prompt:
URL slugs for the docs CMS. Marketing keeps pasting titles like "Café au Lait — Top 10 Tips (2026)!" and our current slugger (a single regex someone wrote in 2019) produces double hyphens, leading dashes, and once emitted an empty string that took down the router. Also two posts titled the same thing silently overwrote each other. Fix all of it in `slugger.py`.

`slugify(text, max_length=None)`:
- lowercase; fold accented latin to plain ascii (café→cafe, smörgåsbord→smorgasbord — unicodedata NFKD plus dropping combining marks gets you most of the way). Handle the stubborn ones that don't decompose: ß→ss, æ→ae, œ→oe, ø→o. Anything else non-latin (CJK, emoji) just drops out.
- every run of anything that isn't a-z or 0-9 (spaces, punctuation, underscores, whatever) becomes ONE hyphen; no leading/trailing hyphens ever. Nothing left → return "".
- max_length: don't cut words in half — if the plain truncation would land mid-word, back up to the previous hyphen; a single word longer than the limit gets a hard cut. Never end in a hyphen. max_length < 1 is a ValueError.

`SlugRegistry(reserved=())` — hands out UNIQUE slugs:
- `assign(title)` slugifies and returns the slug, remembering it. First taker gets the bare slug; later collisions get -2, -3, ... in order. Careful: a title whose own slug is "my-report-2" while that slug is taken becomes "my-report-2-2", and it must NOT derail the counter for "my-report".
- reserved words (exact whole-slug matches, like "admin" or "api") are never handed out bare — first requester gets -2. Slugs merely starting with a reserved word are fine.
- empty slugs fall back to base "untitled" and take suffixes like anything else.
- separate registries don't share state.

`python3 test_slugger.py` decides; hands off the tests. Stdlib only (unicodedata encouraged).
